title: rosa canina subsp. hirtella
intro: Rosa canina subsp. hirtella, also known as the prickly wild rose or hirtella rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa canina plant, a deciduous shrub native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its high content of vitamins C and E, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Rosa canina subsp. hirtella include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to soothe digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
